page: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 45 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 61.0 GB of memory, providing 7.62 GB per core.

This instance includes a bundled 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for high-capacity local storage needs. For network connectivity, it offers a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate modest compute capabilities. The single-core stress-ng performance is 883.49, and the multi-core score is 4158.51, both falling into lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth is mixed, with read-only large block operations reaching an average of 41.72 GB/sec, while write operations are weaker at 23.58 GB/sec. Database operations, such as Redis SET, perform at 2.37 million operations per second.

The i3.2xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require high local storage density and moderate memory capacity rather than raw processing power. It is suitable for NoSQL databases, transactional data stores, and log analytics. It is less suited for compute-heavy tasks or high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. It features 61.0 GB of memory and a bundled 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, operating under the Xen hypervisor with a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows modest compute and memory write performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 893.0 and a multi-core score of 3348.0. Large-block memory reads achieve an average throughput of 41.72 GB/sec, while database operations like Redis SET perform at 2.37 million operations per second. This instance represents a qualitative tradeoff, prioritizing high-capacity local storage over raw processing speed. It is best suited for storage-intensive workloads, including NoSQL databases, transactional data stores, and log analytics, rather than compute-bound applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of RAM, and a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD. It is designed for low-latency database and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of RAM, and a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory for data-intensive applications.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
61.0 GB of system memory yielding 7.62 GB per core
Optimized for NoSQL databases, log processing, and transactional data stores
